title: NOC 21311 – Computer Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Computer Engineers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by growth in telecommunications infrastructure, semiconductor manufacturing, hardware R&D, data-network expansion, and advanced electronics. Classified under NOC 21311 (TEER 1), this occupation is well-suited for:

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Analyze system requirements and design architecture
Develop microprocessors, circuit boards, and semiconductor devices
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer, electrical, or electronics engineering
job_outlook: This keeps Computer Engineering a priority-relevant occupation for immigration—especially for professionals experienced in:
additional_information: Canada’s telecom firms, hardware manufacturers, network providers, and IT-consulting companies continue to recruit Computer Engineers for:
Semiconductor & chip designNetwork infrastructureFibre-optic systemsData-communications architectureEmbedded hardware platforms
Annual earnings typically range from $49,800–$125,300 CAD, depending on province, licensing status, and specialization.
